Crime Data cleaning and visualization¶

Cleaning a messy US Crime Data named "crime_messy_data" for Reliable Trend Analysis.¶

my goals in this project are¶

  • remove duplicate rows
  • standardizing categorical variables
  • fixing inconsistent capitalization
  • covert data types
  • parsing date and time columns
  • detect outlier
  • visualize the data

Importing necessary libraries¶

In [1]:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

Loading the dataset¶

In [6]:
file_path = "crime_incidents_messy.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(file_path)

Cleaning the various columns¶

In [17]:
df['incident_id'] = df['incident_id'].str.replace('/', '').str.replace('%', '')
print(df['incident_id'])

0       INC001115
1       INC004706
2       INC002249
3       INC000021
4       INC000488
          ...    
5045    INC003247
5046    INC000314
5047    INC002761
5048    INC001022
5049    INC003216
Name: incident_id, Length: 5050, dtype: object
In [18]:
df['crime_type'] = df.crime_type.str.replace('/', '').str.replace('%', '').str.replace('.','')
df['crime_type'] = df.crime_type.str.lower()
In [19]:
pd.set_option('display.max_rows', None)
In [20]:
print(df['crime_type'].unique())

['asslt' 'burglary' 'homocide' 'property damage' 'domestc violence'
 'breaking & entering' 'robbery' 'manslaughter' 'b&e' 'narcotics'
 'burglry' 'theft' 'fraudulent activity' 'drug offense' 'armed robbery'
 'arsen' 'abduction' 'graffiti' 'trespass' 'cyber crime' 'trespassing'
 'sexual assault' 'fraud' 'theftlarceny' 'larceny' 'arson' 'homicide'
 'tresspassing' 'cybercrime' 'hacking' 'assault' 'sexual assualt'
 'fire setting' 'drug offence' 'drunk driving' 'duii' 'robbry' 'vandalism'
 'vandlism' 'murder' 'kidnaping' 'kidnapping' 'dui' 'roberry'
 'assault & battery' 'dv' '  graffiti  ' 'domestic violence' 'scam'
 'sex assault' 'dom violence' 'sa' 'deception' '  battery  ' 'dwi' 'drugs'
 'online fraud' 'stealing' '  robbery  ' 'battery' '  dui  ' 'theft '
 '  narcotics  ' '  vandalism  ' '  sexual assault  ' '  duii  '
 'assault  &  battery' '  burglary  ' 'sexual  assualt' 'sexual  assault'
 '  larceny  ' '  trespassing  ' 'drug  offense' 'sex  assault'
 '  kidnaping  ' '  trespass  ' 'armed  robbery' 'domestic  violence'
 '  theft  ' '  drug offense  ' '  assault  ' 'property  damage'
 '  stealing  ' '  property damage  ' '  b&e  ' '  dwi  ' '  abduction  '
 '  fraudulent activity  ' '  dom violence  ' '  cybercrime  ' 'theft  '
 '  drug offence  ' '  sexual assualt  ' '  hacking  ' '  fraud  '
 'drug  offence' '  dv  ' '  sex assault  ' 'cyber  crime'
 '  theftlarceny  ']
In [21]:
df['crime_type'] = df.crime_type.str.replace(r'\s+', '', regex=True)
In [22]:
clean_map = {
    # Homicide
    'homocide': 'homicide', 'murder': 'homicide', 'manslaughter': 'homicide',
    
    # Theft/Robbery
    'robbry': 'robbery', 'roberry': 'robbery', '  robbery  ': 'robbery',
    'armed  robbery': 'armed robbery', 'stealing': 'theft', 'larceny': 'theft',
    'theft/larceny': 'theft', 'theft ': 'theft',
    
    # Assault & Battery
    'asslt': 'assault', 'assault & battery': 'assault/battery', 
    'assault  &  battery': 'assault/battery', 'battery': 'assault/battery',
    
    # Drugs/DUI
    'drug offense': 'narcotics', 'drug offence': 'narcotics', 'drugs': 'narcotics',
    'duii': 'dui', 'd.u.i.': 'dui', 'dwi': 'dui',
    
    # Burglary/Trespass
    'burglry': 'burglary', 'breaking & entering': 'burglary', 'b&e': 'burglary',
    'tresspassing': 'trespassing', 'trespass': 'trespassing',
    
    # Domestic Violence
    'domestc violence': 'domestic violence', 'dom. violence': 'domestic violence',
    'dv': 'domestic violence',
    
    # Cyber/Fraud
    'cyber crime': 'cybercrime', 'hacking': 'cybercrime',
    'fraudulent activity': 'fraud', 'scam': 'fraud', 'online fraud': 'fraud',
    'deception': 'fraud',
    
    # Vandalism/Arson
    'vandlism': 'vandalism', 'graffiti': 'vandalism',
    'arsen': 'arson', 'fire setting': 'arson',
    
    # Sexual Assault
    'sexual assualt': 'sexual assault', 'sa': 'sexual assault', 'sex assault': 'sexual assault',
    
    # Kidnapping
    'kidnaping': 'kidnapping'
}

# Apply the mapping
df['crime_type'] = df['crime_type'].replace(clean_map)
In [ ]:
df['crime_type'] = df.crime_type.str.replace('theflarceny','larceny').str.replace('assault/battery','battery')
    .str.replace('sexassault','sexualassualt')
In [ ]:
cleaner_map = {'theftlarceny':'larceny','assault/battery':'battery','sexassault':'sexualassualt','sexualassualt':'sexualassault',
               'duii':'dui','dv':'domviolence','fraudulentactivity':'fraud'}
df['crime_type'] = df['crime_type'].replace(cleaner_map)
In [ ]:
## Defining the mapping to resolve tautologies
crime_mapping = {
    # Consolidating synonyms and spelling variants
    'stealing': 'theft',
    'larceny': 'theft',
    'kidnaping': 'kidnapping',
    'abduction': 'kidnapping',
    'domviolence': 'domesticviolence',
    'drugoffence': 'drugoffense',
    'narcotics': 'drugoffense',
    'trespass': 'trespassing',
    'graffiti': 'vandalism',
    'b&e': 'burglary',
    
    # Standardizing impaired driving
    'dui': 'drunkdriving',
    'dwi': 'drunkdriving',
    
    # Collapsing specific crimes into broader categories
    'hacking': 'cybercrime',
    'armedrobbery': 'robbery'
}

# Applying the fix
df['crime_type'] = df['crime_type'].replace(crime_mapping)
In [ ]:
print(df['crime_type'].unique())
In [ ]:
print(df['district'].unique())
In [ ]:
# 1. Standardize formatting: Remove whitespace and make everything lowercase
df['district'] = df.district.str.strip().str.lower()

# 2. Define a mapping for abbreviations and shorthand
mapping = {
    'sou': 'south',
    'nor': 'north',
    'eas': 'east',
    'wes': 'west',
    'cen': 'central',
    'mid': 'midtown'
}

# 3. Apply mapping and capitalize for readability
# We use replace with a regex-like logic or simply map the shorthand
df['district'] = df['district'].replace(mapping)
In [ ]:
print(df['district'].unique())
In [ ]:
print(df['city'].value_counts())
In [ ]:
print(df['city'].unique())
In [ ]:
df.head()
In [ ]:
print(df['state'].unique())
In [ ]:
state_map = {
    'GA': 'Georgia',
    'OH': 'Ohio',
    'AZ': 'Arizona',
    'PA': 'Pennsylvania',
    'CA': 'California',
    'TX': 'Texas',
    'IL': 'Illinois',
    'NY': 'New York',
    'FL': 'Florida',
    'NC': 'North Carolina'
}
df['state'] = df['state'].replace(state_map)

print(df['state'].unique())

df.head()

df.to_csv('crimecleaned.csv')

import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('crimecleaned.csv')
In [35]:
df = pd.read_csv('crimecleaned.csv')
In [ ]:
df.info()
In [ ]:
print(df.isnull().sum())
In [39]:
#xtracting and converting to numeric then validating and replacing the missing ones 
cols = ['latitude', 'longitude']
df[cols] = df[cols].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors='coerce')
valid_lat = df['latitude'].between(-90, 90)
valid_lon = df['longitude'].between(-180, 180)
df[cols] = df[cols][valid_lat & valid_lon].copy()
In [ ]:
print(df[cols].value_counts())
In [41]:
df.victim_first_name = df.victim_first_name.fillna('unknown')
df.victim_last_name =df.victim_last_name.fillna('unknown')
In [42]:
df.suspect_first_name = df.suspect_first_name.fillna('unknown')
df.suspect_last_name = df.suspect_last_name.fillna('unknown')
In [ ]:
print(df.isnull().sum())
In [45]:
severity_map = {'1':'low','2':'medium','3':'high','4':'critical'}
df['severity'] = df['severity'].replace(severity_map)
In [49]:
df['severity'].unique()
df['severity'] = df['severity'].replace ( {'Crit':'critical','med':'medium','NaN':'unknown'})
df['severity'] = df['severity'].str.lower()
df['severity'] = df['severity'].fillna('unknown')
In [50]:
df['severity'].unique()
Out[50]:
array(['medium', 'high', 'low', 'unknown', 'critical'], dtype=object)
In [51]:
df['victim_gender'].fillna('unknown')
df['victim_gender'] =df['victim_gender'].fillna('unknown')
df['victim_gender'] = df['victim_gender'].str.lower()
df['victim_gender'] = df['victim_gender'].replace ( {'m':'male','f':'female'})
In [52]:
df['suspect_gender'] = df['suspect_gender'].fillna('unknown')
df['suspect_gender'] =df['suspect_gender'].str.lower()
df['suspect_gender'] = df['suspect_gender'].replace ( {'m':'male','f':'female'})
In [57]:
df['badge_number'] = df['badge_number'].fillna('N/A')
df['badge_number'] = pd.to_numeric(df['badge_number'], errors='coerce').fillna(0).astype(int)
df['suspect_id'] = df['suspect_id'].fillna('unknown')
df['victim_id'] = df['victim_id'].fillna('unknown')
df['suspect_age'] = df['suspect_age'].abs()
df['victim_age'] = df['victim_age'].abs()
df['suspect_race'] = df['suspect_race'].fillna('unknown')
In [ ]:
print(df.isnull().sum())
In [60]:
df.to_csv('crimecleaned4.csv', index=False)

df['weapon_used'] = df['weapon_used'].str.lower()

In [24]:
df.case_status.unique()
df.case_status = df.case_status.str.lower()

df.case_status = df.case_status.fillna('not reported')

In [50]:
df.resolution.unique()
df.resolution = df.resolution.str.lower()
map = {'arres made':'arrest made','warning':'warning issued','case dismissed':'dismissed',' nan':'unkown'}
df.resolution = df.resolution.replace(map)
df.resolution= df['resolution'].fillna('unkown')

df.num_arrests = df.num_arrests.astype('Int64')
df.num_arrests= df.num_arrests.abs()

df.num_arrests = df.num_arrests.fillna(0)
df.num_arrests.value_counts()
In [61]:
df.property_loss_usd= pd.to_numeric(df['property_loss_usd'], errors= 'coerce').abs()
df.property_loss_usd = df.property_loss_usd.fillna(0)

df.properity_loss_usd = df.property_loss_usd.round(0)

df.property_loss_usd.value_counts()
In [69]:
df.reported_online = df.reported_online.str.lower().str.strip()
df.reported_online = df.reported_online.replace(['0','1'],['yes','no'])
df.reported_online = df.reported_online.replace(['true','false'],['yes','no'])
df.reported_online = df.reported_online.fillna('unknown')

Converting date column to proper datetime format¶

In [100]:
df.incident_datetime = pd.to_datetime(df['incident_datetime'], errors='coerce')
In [111]:
df.incident_datetime.unique()
Out[111]:
<DatetimeArray>
['2024-04-16 08:45:03', '2022-01-11 22:03:29', '2022-04-14 11:39:24',
 '2020-12-09 15:14:24', '2021-07-24 20:08:13',                 'NaT',
 '2021-10-03 05:17:02', '2023-09-30 14:49:01', '2021-01-22 02:37:24',
 '2021-03-29 04:16:18',
 ...
 '2022-12-13 16:49:02', '2023-11-26 16:27:41', '2019-12-04 03:38:33',
 '2024-01-06 16:30:04', '2020-02-19 05:09:52', '2020-03-25 05:55:21',
 '2021-12-18 17:27:18', '2023-06-23 04:09:19', '2018-04-25 14:38:24',
 '2023-03-29 09:54:50']
Length: 4317, dtype: datetime64[ns]
In [107]:
df['incident_datetime'].isna().mean()
Out[107]:
np.float64(0.13722772277227724)
In [120]:
df = df.dropna(subset=['incident_datetime'])
In [121]:
df=df.reset_index(drop=True)

Data Visualization¶

This is a series of charts to visualize the cleaned crime dataset¶

In [44]:
plt.figure(figsize=(20, 8))
sns.countplot(data=df,x='crime_type');
plt.title('crime_frequency');
plt.xlabel('crime_type');
plt.ylabel('frequency');
In [42]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
sns.countplot(x='weapon_used', data=df)
plt.title('weapon frequency')
plt.xlabel('weapon')
plt.ylabel('frequency')
plt.show()

Histogram¶

In [39]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.hist(df["victim_age"])

    # Title to the plot
plt.title("victim age count")
plt.show()
In [19]:
labels = ['arrest made ', 'warning issued', 'unknown ', 'no arrest ','dismissed']
sizes = [1209,808, 792, 776, 772] 
colors = ['#66b3ff','#ff9999','#99ff99','#ffcc99','#c2c2f0','#ffb3e6']
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 7)) 
plt.pie(sizes, 
        labels=labels, 
        autopct='%1.1f%%', 
        startangle=140, 
        colors=colors,
        pctdistance=0.85) 
plt.axis('equal') 
plt.title("Case Resolution Status")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [46]:
plt.figure(figsize=(20, 5))
sns.boxplot(data=df, x='district' ,y= 'crime_type' );

plt.pie(df.case_status.value_counts(),labels =df.case_status.value_counts().index,wedgeprops = {'width':0.5});
In [26]:
import seaborn as sns
plt.figure(figsize=(20, 7))
plt.title("weapon used vs severity")
plt.tight_layout()
sns.countplot(data=df, x='weapon_used' ,hue= 'severity' );
In [27]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))
sns.scatterplot(data=df, 
                x='longitude', 
                y='latitude', 
                hue='crime_type', 
                alpha=0.5, 
                palette='viridis',
                s=20) 
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left', title='Crime Type')
plt.title('Spatial Distribution of Crimes by Type', fontsize=15)
plt.xlabel('Longitude')
plt.ylabel('Latitude')

plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.6) 
plt.show()
In [36]:
success_stats = df.groupby('crime_type').agg(
    Total_Incidents=('incident_id', 'count'),
    Total_Arrests=('num_arrests', 'sum')
).sort_values(by='Total_Incidents', ascending=False)

success_stats.plot(kind='bar', 
                   figsize=(14, 7), 
                   color=['#4C72B0', '#55A868'], 
                   width=0.8)
plt.title('Crime Count vs. Successful Arrests by Category', fontsize=16)
plt.xlabel('Crime Type', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Count', fontsize=12)
plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha='right')
plt.legend(['Total Incidents', 'Total Arrests'])
plt.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [37]:
df.to_csv('finishedcrime.csv', index=False)

df.to_excel('crimecleaned7.xlsx', index=False)
